question: I want to report a fraud.
answer: We take all fraud reporting very seriously and ensure prompt handling for all such cases. Request you to reset and change all your PINs and Passwords immediately. No executive from Bajaj Pay will ever ask for OTP or any PIN/Passwords. Request you to ensure that you do not disclose any such sensitive information to anyone. Please access the Passbook section in the App or Website and select the Transaction and click on "Report a fraud" in the Help & Support section

question: How to raise service request?.
answer:
You can raise a request with us by clicking below link. Our representative will respond you within 48 hours. If you have already raised a request with us you can check the status by following mentioned steps:1. Go to home page
2. Click on Menu button in right bottom corner
3. Scroll down to Help and support
4. Click on 'Click here to view all raised requests'. CTA Label: Raise a request, Android CTA link: bajajsuperapp://helpsupport/raisedrequest, IOS CTA link: bajajsuperapp://helpsupport/raisedrequest,  Web CTA link:https://bajajfinserv.in/myaccountlogin?Source=RAR

question: I have already raised a request with you/please help to share status of my already raised request
answer:
If you have already raised a request with us you can check the status by following mentioned steps:
1. Go to home page
2. Click on Menu button in right bottom corner
3. Scroll down to Help and support
4. Click on "Click here to view all raised requests"

question: How can I use this app
answer:
You can use various features of our app:
You can make any bill payments, use wallet or UPI mode through our app for making payments.

question: Why am I not receiving an OTP while setting up UPI pin?
answer: If you are not receiving an OTP while setting up a UPI PIN, it could be due to a network issue or a delay at the bank's end in sending the OTP SMS.

question: How do I send money to a bank account?
answer: In the UPI section, you can use the "send to bank" feature, where you will need to enter the bank account number and IFSC code of the payee to initiate the transfer.

question: How do I revoke an AutoPay I have authorised using Bajaj Finserv App on an external merchant platform?
answer: A UPI mandate cannot be revoked by user if it is Loan or insurance related, for this you need to contact the biller/merchant directly. If the merchant is Bajaj, request you to click on the menu button below and select relevant option to get resolution. If bill payment related autopay, you can revoke directly by going UPI settings by clicking UPI under Homepage and then clicking on revoke Autopay post selecting the relevant Autopay.

question: How do I modify an AutoPay I have authorised using Bajaj Finserv App on an external merchant platform?
answer: A UPI mandate cannot be modify by user if it is Loan or insurance related, for this you need to contact the biller/merchant directly. If the merchant is Bajaj, request you to click on the menu button below and select relevant option to get resolution. If bill payment related autopay, you can revoke directly by going UPI settings by clicking UPI under Homepage and then clicking on modify Autopay post selecting the relevant Autopay.

question: How do I pause/unpause an AutoPay I have authorised using Bajaj Finserv App on an external merchant platform?
answer: A UPI mandate cannot be paused by user if it is Loan or insurance related, for this you need to contact the biller/merchant directly. If the merchant is Bajaj, request you to click on the menu button below and select relevant option to get resolution. If bill payment related autopay, you can revoke directly by going UPI settings by clicking UPI under Homepage and then clicking on modify Autopay post selecting the relevant Autopay.

question: What are reccurance pattern for each mandate?
answer: All the mandates have a frequency of execution, and the frequency is defined as recurrence pattern for each mandate. The frequency will be setup by the initiating PSP for mandates as per the user/merchant requirement basis confirmation from the user.   “Recurrence Value” parameter is required to be referred in the API for applicability of the executions as per the defined recurrence patterns.   Below are the types of possible recurrence patterns-   1. One time   2. Daily   3. Weekly   4. Fortnightly   5. Monthly   6. Bi-monthly   7. Quarterly   8. Halif-yearly   9. Yearly   10. As presented: As Presented recurrence pattern refers execution of mandate by the payer PSP whenever Payee PSP presents for execution.

question: What is UPI International?
answer: UPI International is a new feature where a user can make a payment outside india using Indian Bank account. Payments can be made through UPI QR and Collect request. It is not live currently.

question: In which countries can I use this UPI International?
answer: Below are the list of countries where UPI is acceptable.  - UAE  - Singapore  - Bhutan  - Nepal  - France.

question: How many transactions can I do from my UPI
answer: You can make 20 person to person transactions per day to same payee, limit will reset within 24 hours.There is no daily limit on merchant transactions

question: What is limit of using UPI as payment mode?
answer: You can make 20 person to person transactions per day to same payee, limit will reset within 24 hours.There is no daily limit on merchant transactions

question: Why is my money deducted for failed payments?
answer: When a payment is made, the amount is debited from one bank account and it is credited to another bank account. However, sometimes the amount is debited but not credited due to issues such as bank server problems, network issues, or delayed responses from the bank. We apologise for any inconvenience caused in such cases.

question: Will I be charged for activating additional UPI IDs?
answer: No, there are no charges for activating additional UPI IDs. However, there is a maximum limit of 3 UPI IDs.

question: What can I do if my bank account is not getting linked?
answer: If you are facing issues in linking your bank account, please check with your bank to verify if they are blocking the account from registering for the UPI.

question: How can I change my Bajaj Pay UPI ID?
answer: In the UPI section of the Bajaj Finserv App, you can manage your profile and create up to 3 UPI IDs. While making payments, you can use the default UPI ID, but any of the 3 can be used to receive payments.

question: How can I deactivate my Bajaj Pay UPI ID?
answer: To deactivate or de-register your UPI ID, please follow the below-given steps: 1. Click on the 'UPI' section of the Bajaj Finserv App 2. Click on 'Profile Manager' 3. Click on the option to de-register your UPI ID

question: What is the process of creating or changing a Bajaj Pay UPI PIN?
answer: To change your Bajaj Pay UPI PIN, please follow the below-given steps: 1. Click on the 'UPI' section of the Bajaj Finserv App 2. Click on 'Profile Manager' 3. Scroll down to your bank account(s), and find the option to change your UPI PIN If you are registering your UPI for the first time, our app will guide you through the process of creating a UPI PIN using your debit card details and OTP authentication.

question: How can I update my mobile number?
answer: We regret to inform you that this functionality is currently not available. Rest assured, we will update you once it becomes live.

question: Can I set up my UPI PIN if I don’t have a debit card?
answer: No, debit card details are required for creating a UPI PIN.

question: Why am I not receiving an OTP while setting up my UPI PIN?
answer: If you are not receiving an OTP while setting up your UPI PIN, it could be due to a network issue or because of some delay at the bank's end in sending the OTP SMS. We recommend you try again after some time.

question: How can I send money using a phone number, UPI ID or UPI number?
answer: To send money using a phone number or UPI ID, please follow these simple steps: 1. Click on the 'UPI' section of the Bajaj Finserv App 2. You will see the option to 'Pay to a Contact' using their phone number or UPI number or send money to a bank account or UPI ID 3. Select your preferred mode and fill in the details to send money

question: Why did my UPI payment fail?
answer: There are multiple reasons why your UPI payment might have failed. Some common reasons include entering the wrong PIN, insufficient balance in the payer's account, the payer/payee's account being frozen, blocked, or blacklisted, technical issues such as delayed bank response or network problems, or certain transaction types not being allowed.

question: Please Resolve the issue of my UPI transaction failing repeatedly.
answer: There are multiple reasons why your UPI payment might have failed. Some common reasons include entering the wrong PIN, insufficient balance in the payer's account, the payer/payee's account being frozen, blocked, or blacklisted, technical issues such as delayed bank response or network problems, or certain transaction types not being allowed.

question: Why is my UPI payment pending?
answer: Your UPI payment must be facing some technical issues. However, we recommend you wait for 48 hours to know the actual status of your payment.

question: How do I request money from someone?
answer: To request money from someone, go to the manage profile page in the UPI section, click on "Payment Request," and then use the "Request" button in the Sent tab. You can search for the payee using their UPI ID or mobile number.
